title: Inclusive Interaction, Content, Responsive Design, and Human Evaluation · Claim Audit: Human Accessibility Eval
summary: The primary flow works by mouse at one viewport but loses focus, status, meaning, readability, and error recovery for changed devices and users. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pp1 human accessibility eval increment in accessible responsive product experience. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defe…
objectives: Combine automated checks, keyboard review, screen-reader or structured-text inspection, zoom, contrast, and affected-user evidence with documented limits.
Provide text alternatives, captions, labels, non-color meaning, reduced motion, adequate targets, and tolerant input.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Human Accessibility Eval · Theory Human Accessibility Eval
steps.instruction:
theory case — The primary workflow works by mouse at one desktop width but loses reading order, focus, status, error recovery, and meaning under changed access needs. The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Human Accessibility Eval contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Human Accessibility Eval · Theory Human Accessibility Eval.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for combine automated checks, keyboard review, screen-reader or structured-text inspection, zoom, contrast, and affected-user evidence with documented limits. For Predict Human Accessibility Eval · Theory Human Accessibility Eval, keep Human Accessibility Eval connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: The primary workflow works by mouse at one desktop width but loses reading order, focus, status, error recovery, and meaning under changed access needs. The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Human Accessibility Eval condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: SLSA specification — Controls source and build tracks, provenance, artifact identity, verification, and honest supply-chain claims.

hints: For Predict Human Accessibility Eval · Theory Human Accessibility Eval, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Human Accessibility Eval precondition, or invariant that must hold.

The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

For Predict Human Accessibility Eval · Theory Human Accessibility Eval, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: One accessibility tool can certify WCAG conformance.

options.text: One accessibility tool can certify WCAG conformance. That claim fails the predict human accessibility eval evidence boundary for Human Accessibility Eval.

For predict human accessibility eval, apply Human Accessibility Eval without its stated preconditions, omit the accessible responsive product experience trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Human Accessibility Eval for accessible responsive product experience.

Combine automated checks, keyboard review, screen-reader or structured-text inspection, zoom, contrast, and affected-user evidence with documented limits. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

content.text: Combine automated checks, keyboard review, screen-reader or structured-text inspection, zoom, contrast, and affected-user evidence with documented limits. content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Human Accessibility Eval while producing accessible responsive product experience.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: The primary workflow works by mouse at one desktop width but loses reading order, focus, status, error recovery, and meaning under changed access needs. The worked example variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Combine automated checks, keyboard review, screen-reader or structured-text inspection, zoom, contrast, and affected-user evidence with documented limits. A defensible solution applies Human Accessibility Eval, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to accessible responsive product experience.

content.code:
# Worked focus: pp1-human-accessibility-eval — Combine automated checks, keyboard review, screen-reader or structured-text inspection, zoom, contrast, and affected-user evidence with documented limits.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-5
focus: pp1-human-accessibility-eval
decision-evidence: Combine automated checks, keyboard review, screen-reader or structured-text inspection, zoom, contrast, and affected-user evidence with documented limits.
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
